text:
ians, mathematicians, decorators, experts in the prepara-
tion of elixirs of life, artisans, (such as carpenters, masons, black-
smiths and potters), spies, jugglers, infants, poets, imposters, tale-
bearers, those engaged in black magic, messengers, eunuchs, buffoons,
those that are proficient in demonology and magic, policemen,
actors, dancers, ghee, oil, oil-seeds, bitter things, observers of vows
(like Brahmacarins), chemists (who prepare good tonics) and
mules.
[Kau'dki river may be the modern Kusi flowing into the
Gariga through the district of Purnea in Bihar, or the Kusiara of
Sylhet (Bangla Desh) flowing through the area known as Panca-
khanda.
Gambhird, mentioned by Kalidasa in the Meghaduta (I. 40),
is a tributary of the Yamuna above the Chambal flowing east from
Gangapur through eastern Rajputana.
